reviews.review_text: The game is great, but the development team sucks. Only adding cosmetic updates with not real game content. It's all PVE on almost every server. I have almost 7000 hours in this game so I'm qualified to tell you that this game has not had any real content update since .7 in beta stage. It's been out 8 years. The dev team gives an update, later takes away the update, and then gives the same update back as a different update and acts like we are supposed to be amazed at their progress.

As an old player who has been playing SCUM since [September 18, 2024], I wrote this text today not to vent my emotions, but to record a disappointing official interactive experience.

[The cause of the incident]

Just a few days ago, I found that my SCUM account was banned. My first reaction was confusion, because I had never used any cheating software and had not violated the rules of the game. I contacted the official support team of SCUM (Bohemia Interactive Support) at the first time to try to figure out what happened.

[Official response]

The reply of the official customer service is very fast, but also very "fatal". They confirmed that my account was mistakenly blocked by the "old version of the anti-cheating system". That is to say, I am innocent and it is the fault of the system.

However, the next sentence made me completely cold: "We no longer use the anti-cheating scheme, so we can't access the relevant records, and we can't modify or revoke the ban."

[Absurd Reality]

It's like the police caught the wrong person and locked you up for several years. When you complained, the police station told you: "The case handling system has been scrapped and the files have been burned, so we can't prove that you are innocent, and you can only continue to be locked up."

Because the official system was replaced, the misjudgment of the old system became a "permanent case". They admit their mistakes, but refuse to correct them.

[My loss]

It's not just a matter of an account. On this account, I invested hundreds of hours to build a base, accumulate equipment, and even invest real money to buy assets in the game. Now, because of the technical iteration of the official system, all this has been cleared. What's more ironic is that I'm not even qualified to "complain", because the judge who judged me (the old system) has "died", and no one can overturn its judgment.

[Concluding Words and Appeals]

I understand that game development needs to update technology, but I can't accept that players pay for the "technical debt" in full. If the official can erase the player's historical data because the system is disabled, then where is the player's trust in the game?

I hope the SCUM team can see this article. Since you can't unblock your account, please at least face your mistake:

1. Please issue an announcement on the official website to inform players that the old version of the anti-cheating system does have the risk of being blocked by mistake.

2. For those players who are mistakenly blocked due to the old system, some in-game compensation (such as props, gold coins, etc.) will be given, instead of a cold sentence of "powerlessness".

Our players support not only the game itself, but also the sincerity of the developer. I hope the official can give a reasonable explanation instead of letting us leave with regret.

Player: [每当JK慢慢脱离她的裤腿]

The development process is just too slow. SCUM took so much time to get where it is that everything now feels clunky and outdated. It has lost its original vision, and instead of fixing core issues, progress just feels stagnant. It’s hard to recommend a game that feels old before it’s even fully finished.

In terms of Survival/Scavenger/Shooter/PVE/Zombies this is the BEST game out there in terms of detail and content, just surviving is fun, nutrients system, detailed injury and sickness system. Just wish they focused more into the single player, Player vs AI aspect, more geared AI running around the map or even AI bases.
Because like in any Online PVP game right now the cheaters ruins everything. Focus more in Single Player/Coop experiences.

This game is not for everyone. If you like survival games with survival tasks such as eating, drinking, using the toilet, and resting then this is a good one. The developers regularly update it which is good, but each update is followed by hotfixes to fix things the updates broke. Even with that being a reality, this is currently my favorite game. The world is huge and there's plenty to do.

I followed the game almost from the beginning and i realy loved it but i have the feeling that it´s one step forward and five steps backward. A lot of work needs to be done and i dont think another cosmetic DLC is the answer. I maybe come back from time to time but as it stands safe your Money and replay Skyrim for the 1000 time or so.
This game releases more DLC than good updates. 6 months into 1.0 update and the game still feels like early access. Garbage devs

Genuinely only a hour in and this game is awful. I look at the base building tab only to see every piece of good furniture is locked behind a paywall, even though I've already paid way to much money. Also, survival my ass because half of the players are butt cheek naked and looting up faster than i can even spawn in. If i wanted to play rust, i could've just got that instead of this. So after getting gangbanged in multiplayer i gone to single player. Because personally i prefer PVE. Only to find out its still the same ass I got in multiplayer but just without action happening. When i tell you this game is a walking simulator, it is. You either walk or run through a forest, until you find a couple small cabins which have a couple pieces of paper and thread in them. The lack of zombies are actually impeccable. And no, I had new player protection off, because im already experienced with survival games. But supposedly they think this game is getting played by cheeto fingered toddlers, because in the ENTIRE time i was playing. I SAW 1 ZOMBIE... ONE ZOMBIE IN A ZOMBIE SURVIVAL GAME...  This game is awful

Game is continually updated, but every update breaks more than it fixes.  Hours after a hotfix, I found my game bugging out in ways that render critical situations unplayable.  I've played through plenty of versions, and lots of different servers from low loot, hardcore servers, officials, and have fun PVE servers.  The servers are all fine, but the constant state of breaking the game in an attempt to improve it has made it unreccomendable for me.

Not Recommended

SCUM had everything going for it. The concept is brilliant, the graphics are genuinely good, and the survival systems on paper are some of the most ambitious in the genre. I wanted to love this game. But after enough hours, the limited customization, the constant bugs, and the baffling design decisions wore down my patience completely.

The single biggest problem with SCUM isn't the game — it's the people making it. Almost every feature is either poorly designed, half-finished, or outright broken, and it has been this way for years.

Hunting is a joke. In the early versions, animals actually spawned in the forest and you could spot and stalk them naturally. Now it's been replaced by an absurd mini-game where you hunt for "clues" — droppings or tracks that literally emit a sound — and walk up to each one just to make the next clue appear. It's tedious, immersion-breaking, and a downgrade from what already worked.

The vitamin system is dead weight. It's been in the game for years, cluttering the interface, and to this day it serves no real purpose. Pure UI pollution.

Base building is frustrating. It's difficult, limited, and riddled with clipping issues (grass poking through objects), and generators drone on with constant noise. Add to that the broken clothes-drying system, where garments take absurd, unrealistic amounts of time to dry.

The most important issue: the spawn system has been broken since day one. In a single-player survival game, the enemies — puppets or NPCs — are the experience, and a working spawn system is what makes it fun. The devs never got it right. Not once, not in any version. Instead, they buried it under  countless configuration options and spawn types so convoluted that the community had to write endless tutorials just to make it do anything — most of them based on pure trial and error, because the devs document nothing.

No mod support. I've heard it was promised for the 1.0 release. It never came. Mods might have been the one thing capable of saving this game, and there's still no official support.

And through all of this, the devs keep shipping new pointless features and content while never fixing the broken foundations they already built.

I can't recommend SCUM. Not because an update couldn't fix it — but because at this point the entire dev team would need to be replaced for this to ever become the game it should have been.

Not fun, not immersive. Just forced, stupid gameplay, like urinating. You can only do it when your bladder reaches a certain point --makes sense-- but when it does, there's no button to press to do it, oh and your character knows how to urinate, they just don't know how to unzip their pants before urinating, that's your job, because we're playing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ babies here who forgot to put on their diapers.

I tried ... I really tried to enjoy this game, and yes, it had its good moments years ago. I was hopeful it would improve over time. It has so much potential, but the developers keep ruining it more and more.
My biggest gripe is the enemy spawn system, especially after the Hordes update.

On one hand, hordes can spawn inside recently cleared houses or nearby in towns and fields just a few meters away in areas that were empty a second ago. This is utterly immersion breaking. If you want to see how a proper horde implementation is done, look at Days Gone, which uses simplified AI for distant hordes miles away. Here, the devs simply spawn enemies close to you out of thin air and call it a day. It feels like the devs expected players not to notice.

Armed NPCs can also spawn 10–20 meters behind your back. Suddenly, you get shot from behind by three armed NPCs from a spot that was completely empty two seconds earlier. There’s no believable way they could have moved there in time, yet the devs call it an “ambush feature.” Yeah, right. I call it lazy spawn logic.

Inside POI puppets can spawn 10–20 meters in front of you, in the same room as you, or even directly on top of you, making them impossible to avoid. The last time I saw a spawn system this bad was in Project I.G.I. back in the early 2000s and even this had a higher enemy spawn distance.

On the other hand, wild puppet spawns have been heavily reduced outside of POIs, even in sandbox singleplayer. Fields, meadows, and forests feel completely dead since the Hordes update. (I turned off hordes spawning out of thin air because it’s incredibly broken. Maybe wild puppet spawns rely on horde spawners too,  who knows?)

Doing a sell run to the next trader base has now become utterly boring. There’s no tension anymore, no regular enemy encounters except for the occasional animal spawn. The devs also added a default setting where puppet encounters can already spawn "aggroed" and magically know where you’re hiding, apparently just for giggles. (Thankfully, it can be turned off.)

I spent days searching for server settings to increase wild puppet spawns and discussed it with people online, only to be told to give up because they had also searched through the settings for days and found nothing that could fix it.

I read that these changes were made for server performance reasons. Fine, but then make it optional for server admins with smaller player counts, or for sandbox singleplayer, instead of disabling it altogether.

The same goes for bird spawning. Years ago, birds used to spawn and fly around. You could hunt them and collect feathers for better arrow crafting. Now there’s no way to get feathers in the southern area of the map because they only spawn in hay bales, which do not exist there. Officially the devs removed birds because of performance reasons and collisions with planes. Fine, but again make it optional for smaller servers or singleplayer. Birds could have been set to no clip or no damage to vehicles, instead of removing them completely.

I mostly play single-player now because even the people who ran the server I liked eventually quit. The game kept getting more and more gameplay issues that were never fixed.

I can live with most bugs and glitches, but this entire spawning system has become game breaking. The devs are essentially killing the game with decisions like these. Instead of fixing critical problems, they release another DLC many players didn’t ask for or distract people with community screenshots showing “how cool” the game looks.

Even long-time YouTubers like Raykit have recently voiced concerns and frustration with the game’s recent direction.

It’s frustrating because the game excels in areas like atmosphere, complexity, and visuals, yet one of the most fundamental gameplay systems "spawning" feels unfinished and deeply flawed
After the new update it tells me my GPU is to "bad" for the game and cant play it anymore and steam wont refund the game. Thanks Gamepires youre really a SCUM.

This game was so promising and in truth still has so much potential, but it’s dying. The developers have time and time again ignored our pleas for bug fixes and optimization and instead have given us useless items, bugged weapons, and an even more broken game. I’ve watched this game fall apart since 1.0 released (about a year ago now) and I can’t in good conscience recommend it to anyone anymore. I love the game, I truly do, but I’ve completely lost faith in the people who made it.

I already paid for the game, and now people act like I’m supposed to pay again just to enjoy it on a decent server. The official servers are a mess, optimization is terrible, cheaters are everywhere, vehicle physics are broken, and the loot system feels completely random half the time. Updates keep coming, but the core problems never get fixed.

The map is huge, yet the performance is still awful. FPS drops constantly, PvP is inconsistent, and the grind is excessive. Instead of having fun, the game feels like a second job. The community can be toxic, admins abuse power on private servers, and solo players get punished hard.

And then the funniest part: “If you want a good experience, pay for a private server.” Absolute joke. Buying the game once apparently isn’t enough anymore.
This game is excellent but the "fatal error" crashes literally make it unplayable, unfortunate

Bought the game on Sale, and really wanted to try it. Unfortunately, this game disables multiplayer on Linux because Battle Eye doesnt work with Proton. 
Refunded

I play this in single player mode.

play the game on normal version (probably dx 11 version). 
hit start. FATAL ERROR in loading screen.
try again.
choose dx 12 Version.
play the game .
ALSO GOT FATAL ERROR MID GAME!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! WTH.
check the forum. AND ITS ALREADY THERE SINCE 2022, F.U. DEV.
guess they arent serrious about V1.0.

for anyone who try to tell me to fix this by my self F U. i paid it. the dev is the one who need to fix all of this so it isnt like another scam game. the game should not be broken in the first place. its not even beta state.

the concept is good about complex real life survival thing. but the enemy respawn is so strange though.
one of the best game concept in my oppinion. but the execution still suck EASE.

Jako loše optimizirana igrica da nemožete fixat bog te mazo skopanje ne radi ljepo več dosta dugo godina bitno da ste stavili retardirani treče lice da nemos vidit oko sebe sranje na kvadrat jadno ono šta treba popravit to nista a dodavanje kozmetike u igru to vam ide najbolje dok god se to ne popravi ne mjenjam mišljenje ni ocjenu zalosno za igru koja je vec dugo onlin

I log in after months of not playing the game and it is somehow worse than ever. The zombie ai is terrible I can be hiding in a building not moving and somehow 3 to 4 zombies spawn outside and go straight to my location. They are jankier than ever and the sounds they make have been the same for years. I had some zombies just yelling and yelling because they heard me but I couldn't find them so it was just annoying me with the same 2 sounds they make. I went to a town and there were like 3 zombies floating on the police station. The NPC's lack intelligence and just stand there until you make noise or they see you and they also have aim bot. I had 3 wolves just stand there howling and growling relentlessly for 5 mins until I finally found them and killed them. 

This game needs a better PVE experience but somehow they have made it worse. Also the game is just boring unless you pvp which I don't do so my experience is just trash. The zombies need more variety like a charger or screamer etc. The cars are fun to build up and planes are cool but once you get them, build a base, and get gear there is nothing to work towards. Plus there's nothing to use the cool guns on other than pvp. If you don't like pvp then you aren't going to like this game. 

I do not recommend this game if you are a pve player but if you are a pvp player then go ahead knock yourself out you might have fun with that. Me personally I'm done until they make meaningful pve and ai changes.
unplayable. had this game since release was great to start with, but now every time I load the save have to delete it to play the game this is the reason it is unplayable in its current state.

I really enjoy this game. It is in my top 5 favorite games of all time. If you like challenging, survival and attention to detail this game is packed to the brim with detail not just looks either. It has similar to life mechanics involving everything from having vitamin deficiencies to gun jams to wound infections. The game isnt perfect yet as the devs are adding new stuff constantly and are always aware about game breaking bugs and are very quick to push out hot fixes for them. so in due time i believe this game will become a polished masterpiece but until then i will enjoy it no matter what.

I loved this game from way back before there 1.0 release. At that time it was said once 1.0 will come private servers and mod support. since then we got broken private servers and zero mod support. until they honor and add mod support I will not support this game anymore.

I played less than two hours and returned it. It seems like there is a very fun game in there if you can get over the hurdle of mastering the labyrinthine user interface, playing inventory Tetris across multiple pockets and the ground, and a health system that makes you micromanage pooping. It took me several minutes to figure out how to bandage a wound, and that's with the AI tutor. And when I figured it out, it still took me several minutes to make the next bandage because, after cutting up all my clothing, I still didn't have enough rags. If feel like there's more than enough content in this game to keep players occupied without taking up so much of our time on metabolism and crafting very simple things. So if you're like me and like a game you can play with your dad, one that you can just pick up and play, 7 Days to Die is probably more your thing.

628 HOURS, years of playing and everything is just as broken as day one. Items/vehicles despawn at will. Vehicles that take hours to piece together get destroyed will sitting there, for no apparent reason. Just annoying, alpha stage bugs that should have been fixed years ago, present in the "Retail" version of this game.

It’s like the developers saw everything right and wrong other games of the same genera were doing, said f’..k it, we’re gonna learn nothing from those games, and made this. If you like getting kicked in the nuts over and over for the most simple things, this is the game for you. Mind you this game has been in early access since 2018, and it released in 2025.  I really tried to like this game, but it’s just so poorly made. This game has some mechanics like are really appealing to me, things like clothing getting wet, stomach food system, and weather conditions, it had so much potential, but it’s clear the devs have no clue how to fix the most basic things. 
The game as it stands, is pure trash. Firstly, I don’t even know why people would play this as multiplayer, given all the stuff wrong with it. 
I wanted a game for more of a solo survival, and wow, did this thing disappoint. Enemies will spawn randomly behind you at the same place you just passed through. Robots shoot you through buildings and even if you have 10 bandages, you can’t bandage yourself enough to save yourself. 
When you build a base, and  you die, the game still spawns you in a random location. 
Weapons and tools – The weapons are garbage. Even at 50 meters, you can aim with a rifle and your round disappears. No recoil, no real effects. There are items that literally do nothing. Oh you found a pickaxe? You think you can mine rocks or something with it, nope, it’s just a melee weapon and for breaking locks. 
AI – AI will run at you into melee range, even if they have long range weapons, so you just knife them, which is the best way to get rid of AI quietly. Enemy AI and zombies will not engage one another, as if they have some sort of alliance. This includes the robots not taking out any zombies. 
Building – this game has one of the WORST building systems I have seen in the past 15 years. 
Everything you craft is soooooooooo slow. Want to build a wall? Well, carry logs ONE BY ONE, to your little shed. Oh you changed your mind and want to cancel the build? Sorry, any resources you committed to the section of the building are now gone. Want to choose yourself which rags or items you want to contribute to the building process? Too f..king bad, the game will do that for you. Want to deposit into the chest you build? Nah, the game will put it on the ground instead. Not to mention the absolutely terrible building menu and system. Like WTF, it’s just awful. I cannot emphasize just how terrible the building process is. It’s like this was an afterthought put together; rather than a core mechanic of the game. 
Death and Item recovery –
Oh you died and want to go pick up your items? Well the items pretty much de-spawn in about 15 minutes, sometimes even faster. After you die, you respawn someplace random. If you want to roll the dice, you can pay more cash and kill yourself, in hopes of spawning someplace closer to your body. The most recent time, I had full kit, all kinds of stuff on me. Got killed in the airfield. Spawned near it, was like wow, how lucky, ran to my body in about 8 minutes, but woops, nothing there. The body had despawned. 
Food and fatigue – Your dude is constantly hungry or thirsty. Which is amazing, because the game has this supposed complex food system, but in reality, just shove all the food you can into yourself and drink whatever you find. When you freshly spawn, you have good stamina. Then as you run around, you build up fatigue; which, pretty much impacts how quickly your stamina drains and regenerates. As of playing this game for more than 30 hours, the only way to alleviate this is to literally just lay down, and watch this bar fill up that takes more than 30 minutes real life time. Otherwise, good luck running anywhere. 
Inventory – The inventory management is a joke. You have a grid system, but the game is so dumb, if you take something out, say to drink or eat from it, then you want to place it back, it doesn’t automatically rotate it back into how you had it, no no, you have to re-rotate it. You get a bunch of inventory space from your clothing and bag pack, but as soon as you have anything in there, oh your dude is over-encumbered. And this is me picking strength as a high attribute so I could have carry weight. Then guess what, you want take you items to one of the 4 vendors located coincidentally in the middle of f..king nowhere to sell things to? Good luck walking there if you haven’t found a vehicle, because your dude will literally be walking extremely slow in a huge map. 
Speaking of the map, you get one, but you think you can zoom in on it? Nope, not a thing. 
So like I said, if you suffering for no reason other than poor game mechanics, this the game for you.

Fun little game. The developers are constantly adding things. Character creation and development is good and there are quite a few things to do even if your not into PVP. A BIG plus is being able to buy it and then play it, no subscription required. They do have DLCs that can be purchased for mostly cosmetic items and characters. The game has bugs but is way more stable than when in alpha. Honestly this game is the only reason I still have Windows installed on my PC.

Overly convoluted, not my type of survival game, but might be great for others who are patient enough to complete long complicated crafting steps and like the idea of RPG skills in an action survival game. I wont give it a negative review because it is made well for what it is and I am sure there are people who like this kind of depth.

I'd say this game could have been great, but has been stagnant for a while. 
I think the only chance to revitalize it is to have proper mod support - but that will only revive the game if it isn't actually dead yet. I wonder why it is delayed, possibly monetization challenges.

The project was big, some changes didn't work out so well, some did. But... comeon, armed npc's.

The DLC system is a bit awful for new players, but I guess to keep some momentum going development wise. As a new player you'll feel a bit grumpy to be 'bullied' into buying DLC's for things that should be standard. 

Aside of mod support, what would really help the game is Co-op  sandbox mode with hot joining friends, to say max 4 players, as alternative to paid servers, or plain single player sandbox.

This is the best worst game I have ever played. It is almost so much fun, but the developers have really focused on the wrong things. We don't need the most realistic mushroom trip I have ever seen. We need decent loot tables and less invincible zombies. When I spend 12 hours getting a vehicle, it shouldn't randomly blow up and become unrepairable after I lightly tap a tree. All in all not a bad game but could be so much more. I would happily pay about 10-15 dollars for it in its current form.

Way too buggy, constant crashes and disappearing stuff for no reason. Better get Soulmask or even Ark instead of this trash.

Edit: not only their suggestion did not fix any of those issues, their "hotfix" broke the single player save I spent 2 hours in earlier today. Yeah, stay the hell away from this crap game made by crappier devs.
All the things that were promised for this game. A fraction of those things made it into the game. Dont recommend unless you get it cheap. Dont bother

UPD: this was a negative review, but the devs have actually acknowledged it, and as I'll say in the very next sentence, this review should've been a neutral one

I truly wish there was a neutral review option... Because the game is truly amazing, it has something for everyone. 
However, there are major drawbacks for casual players such as myself. First thing first - the saves are NOT working correctly in the single player. Half-build structures disappear, ammo poofs out from the magazines (I've lost 3 SKS clips worth of ammo due to this). There are some random events that leave you absolutely zero chance of survival, such as hordes, groups of armored NPCs and ESPECIALLY the dropship. 
There's also almost no PvE content. The quests are almost all the same, "Find X amount of Y". And in most cases that Y item is sold at that exact trader, so you can just abuse it to hell and back. There are some bunkers and military bases scattered here and there though, which are kinda good. However, the human enemies are inconsistent af. They either oneshot you with a damn basic craftable bow and arrow, or they are missing 100% of their shots. I wish there was some sort of progression of the map, like in Fallout 4, where the enemies get progressivelly stronger the further away you are from the starting area. It'd also be great if there were some outposts or buildings/towns held by human enemies, or just whatever point of their concentration for you to clear. These would make PvE SO much better, and actually worthwhile (working single-player saves would also bump up the PvE quality quite significantly).
The same lack of progression can be applied to the traders. All of the vehicles' parts are unlocked at exactly 250 fame (the form of progression in this game, alongside money and character attributes). Same goes for the weapons, at a different amount of fame. So there's no reason to get a Wolfswagen instead of a Rager (which is the best vehicle in the game), or to get a bad weapon instead of the good one. The attributes system creator deserves a separate circle of hell just for him alone. It takes WEEKS to get a single attribute to the max value, if you don't want to abuse some AFK strats, that is. But even then, the game forces you to live in it to max out your character. Which is good if you're a no-life ultra super duper hardcore player, that can sacrifice their job for 5 strength alone. And which is bad if you're just a moderately chill player. Before you say it, it's not difficult, it's stupid. Same goes for the actual skills like cooking, it takes forever to level them up.
Now, let's talk about why this review should've been a neutral one: the game is gorgeous. I don't know how they've made it, but the game looks and runs better than pretty much every single other game around. The mechanics are amazingly thought out. As you probably know, the game has a ♥♥♥♥ ton of thoroughly developed mechanics. Such as fishing. You don't just take your rod out and click LMB once to start fishing, and then LMB 2nd time to actually catch the fish. No, there are a ton of factors to think of: weather, type of water body, fishing reel, floaters, different types of bait for different fish, and the process itself is akin to the Far Cry 5, but just better and more complex overall. And that same thought process goes to literally anything you can think of: farming, cooking, healing, all things weapons, literally everything in this game is thought out from the ground up. It may sound scary and difficult, but it isn't, it all comes together and just works!
So in the end, if you're a hardcore player - go for it. But if you're a solo casual player - stay away.

WASTE time of your life. You farm 10 Hours but die easy on one click with easy craft gun even on your max gear. If You want immersive go play other games. this game is not that realistically is a lot of way to cheat with no hack or programming.  It get you a lot of your time (like all day) to make or get some things and you will lost everything in second. Consider time of your life. You can use your brain else where more creative and fun. No point to play. Zombie Sound absolute trash zzz . the game also trash. DO NOT PLAY THIS GAME.

It was working great a few months ago until i tried running the game after a month or two but when i tried running the game it took me to a black screen saying fatal error then the game crashed after that I have tried everything to fix it and nothing has worked so i dont know what is going on but they need to fix whatever it is
After updates it gets stuck on black screen after launch. Unplayable. Was alright before, but not anymore. Thanks, I guess..
I would recco this game. With the right people, it's amazing. It would be weird if I played this for 3027 hours and didn't like it. Right?
I last played this game Jan.12 2024 because at that point, the recent update turned it to ♥♥♥♥. I see nothing has changed over 2 years later.
Wanted to jump back in after the May update, but can't even get it to run. Fatal crash upon starting game, forcing restart of computer. Avoid this frustration until fixed
Great game, some quirks but once you get used to them it works. Easy to spend hours in the game.
I like this game alot but it need full linux support for multiplayer.

This game is absolutely amazing, and there are so many things I love about it, like the environment, graphics, the map, wildlife, the hunting system, ambient sounds, and much more. My friend and I usually play in PVE  and really enjoy our time together.

However, we always feel that this game has much more potential. For instance, the building system is currently quite weak, and there aren’t many objects or items available to craft. For PVE players, the building system is crucial. We need features like a proper electrical wiring system ,not just generators that provide power in a small radius , and an irrigation or plumbing system. There are many other things I could add to this list. If I were to compare the building system with Rust, Rust is much stronger in that area. But even so, I still prefer SCUM and want to see this game reach its full potential. I’m certain that once it does, it will have the highest player count among all survival games.

Finally, I suggest the developers stop releasing so many DLCs. Please don’t be so money-hungry; this approach is ruining the game. We pay for the game once, but then we have to buy various DLCs for something as small as a haircut or minor items. It feels like almost everything you’ve added to the game lately has been locked behind a DLC.
Loooooove !!! Always  wanted to play  Scum since it came out but never could with the computer i had at the time! Now with a working computer im so happy im able to play this game!
Ups and Downs here - Its a great game, but cheaters kill it - the end.
The map so empty. zombie so rare and after 3 hours of gameplay didnt see any animal.

Uninstalled this garbage.

Been playing since it was released. Last few years, every update arguably makes it worse.
There is a complete lack of proper direction for this game, or expanding it so existing players actually have something to look forward to. Would happily have paid for this game over and over if it actually delivered.

It's also unfortunate this game is still on UE4, which means that things like vehicles and zombies are tracked and handled uniquely on your game instance, so different to your squad mates experience. Try driving two vehicles somewhat close to each other...

The lack of actual new content like a new copy-pasted or larger map after nearly 10 years of playing this.
The team behind this currently lacks great project management and vision. Absolute lack of testing of what they release. Or the quests, what is this ♥♥♥♥? It's a proof of concept, not a developed feature. Great job.

Until the release of the new hunting update, which arguably is pretty terrible.
Previously you had to hunt animals by listening for sounds that clearly are nearby - no, they have not spawned until you follow a number of locations, while earlier in the Alpha/beta stage animals spawned randomly and left blood to be tracked in awareness - which was basic, but somewhat realistic to what it was for years.

Just recently your squadmates would get Fatal Error each time you ate or drank in the vicinity of them. Or arrows didn't work, or a meriad of things that weeks later got fixed. It's just not enjoyable any more as-is.

Maybe there is hope for SCUM with the modding support if it ever becomes reliable and stable for the community to put serious efforts into it.

Oh well, you can still have plenty of fun as a new player, sure.
Performance wise, and looks of the game is decent. Improved fairly recently, however at the expense of ruining the zombie spawn completely to the point where its just an annoyance. The addition of NPCs were nice, however they spawn in a predicted fashion, or my favorite, in rooms you just cleared behind you. Love it. If not for their super human accuracy at great distance at night with a shotgun. Not wearing a bullet proof vest? RUN. Once you got the vest, they are as dangerous as zombies.

Played mainly on hardcore servers the past few years, as the baseline difficulty of the game in terms of PvE is too easy and gets boring too quickly.
However there are other drawbacks with hardcore servers, stupid things with vehicles and their fuel consumption.
Motorbikes on hardcore servers require constant refill, without a petrol can you can only drive straight between fuel locations.

So many missed opportunities, otherwise excellent map design and locations ruined by absolutely nothing special about any of them. Like the nuclear area, massive part of the map - nothing at all special besides the reactor.

Have fun on 1/4th of SCUM Island SCUMers!
Hope to be back once the leadership team gets their ♥♥♥♥ together and actually adds functioning content to it.
Anything you would expect to do in this kind of game is currently missing. It had potential, but since it hasn't reached that potential after all this time, I fear it might never happen.

FIX YOUR SERVER CONNECTION ISSUES! Redownloaded this game to play after new update then had to delete all the pak files, and then 2 hours to reverify files  it just to get it to start. Then spent two more hours in the game on google trying to find out why no matter what server i try to connect to i just keep getting connection timed out. Dude im so done. Click game, it opens, choose server and play. THATS ALL I FREAKING WANT! And thats after jumping through all the hoops for the PlaySafe ID crap because all the other servers never even get half full. I can handle in game bugs, crappy cooking and other weird updates, iv been with his game sense waaaay  before it was released. But what i have no patience for in ANY game is buggy tec issues that make it unplayable at all or require hours of messing with files, re-verifying or all the other IT crap, o  or my personal favorite constantly trying to blame it on my wifi or computer or fire wall or what the hell ever else when i literally have only ever had issue with this game. Every time i take a break and re-install or dont play for a while and do updates this crap happens. Please make objects persist on open world solo and do not remove them from gameplay to gameplay. It is game breaking for me. Otherwise this game has true survival teeth. 4 out of 5 because of disappearing items in world.

I like the game, but i died and when i got back only my two weapons were there my body was gone. I looked around it was nowhere nearby. I did not die on the way back. I relogged it was not there. I am in single player and apparently this has been an issues for many years. If this is the case allow us to not drop anything on death in the server settings. If you refuse to fix this major issue or don't know how to stop my corpse with all my items from immediately disappearing just add a setting to keep items on death. I know you don't want to make it too easy by keeping items on death but if the other option of finding my corpse are unavailable because it glitched through the ground just give me the option to keep my items until you eventually fix this issue. I have seen posts from like 6 years ago on this issue when should we expect a fix in the next 12 years? I like the game but dam I wish this could be fixed.

Fun game, can be rough around the edges and some parts are hyperdetailed in terms of mechanic's or features while others are lacking, other than that it chill but can still have some good butt clenching moments so 8.5/10.
Dear developers. Add more vehicles, and add a feature so that you can remove the sidecar from a sidecar motorcycle. Plise!

I play solely PVE, so take that into consideration with this review. I can't speak on the quality of PVP. 

I've met some of the nicest people in this game. The server I'm in has been nothing but helpful, and genuine, and helped me learn the game as well as experience it on my own. They've guided me in the right direction many times. I was struggled in a town with zombies, with just a bat, and a group drove in, mowed them down with machine guns and let me come with them. They gave me a stab-proof vest, as well as a pistol, and helped me learn the fundamentals of combat. It was honestly so much fun, just chilling and travelling in the back of a ute with a bunch of randoms. They're the kinda people that keep me playing games like this. 

Another story, the host of the session saw me struggling, running from one end of the map to the other on foot, and asked if I wanted a car. Gave me one, taught me how to lock it, and went on his way. 

I would recommend this game based on my personal experience if you want to find chill servers and have a good time. Also if you take mushies you start seeing your pistol as a banana and have a lil zombie horde following you around. 

9/10.

Survival mechanics are detailed, map is huge, and there's always something to do, whether that's looting, exploring, crafting, or just trying to stay alive. It has a bit of a learning curve, but once you get the hang of it, it's very rewarding. Still plenty learn, but I'm enjoying it so far.
Good game, its very addictive and adventurous.

I like this game quite a bit. It's a lot like DayZ but a little more forgiving when it comes to acquiring items you need.  I have only played solo so far, and there is still a little challenge especially with mechs walking around, and sometimes the npc players get some lucky shots in.   I have quite enjoyed my time playing solo, exploring new areas, preparing  to take longer trips away from my base. Figuring out how to craft new things etc.   I would recommend this game to anyone that likes survival crafting games. I have even experienced minimal bugs.   2080ti - i99900K 32g ram

I would love more put into the solo version (sandbox) mode.   It runs great, only crashed once so far,  on a boat..... which sucked for obvious reasons.    But id love if the NPC players had a better mix of gear, and especially their own bases to raid, or potentially try to raid mine while online.   Someone else had mentioned this in the reviews and i completely agree with them.    All in all a great game.
Base building, crafting and survival systems are all best of class!  Always on the edge of your seat dealing with mechs, players and the environmental elements.
Its been a lot of fun, very similar to DayZ if you are familiar. Great to play with friends. no complaints.

I have been playing Scum for 3 or 4 years and as pc games go, this one is about all i play. I have watched the progression from a very early stage. The first couple of years I did not have internet so I became very immersed in the single player aspect of the game. I did learn a lot about the early versions by following a few people on youtube. I have since played on quite a few multi player servers, and have very recently purchased the game for my daughter. We have quite the base on a PVE server and she loves the game also. I love the full immersion of the game. I am here to stay.

It's one of the most immersive survival games I’ve played. The level of detail is insane, from metabolism mechanics to base building and PvP. The map is huge and beautiful, and there’s always something to do. Gunplay feels satisfying, and surviving actually feels rewarding. It can be challenging for new players, but once you learn the mechanics, it becomes incredibly fun. The developers keep improving the game, which is great to see. If you enjoy hardcore survival games with depth and realism, Scum is definitely worth trying <3

what i really like of this game is the quality and game interaction,game play,level of difficulty that brings great challenges.what i moslty dislike is the unability of creating a free server of even just play a coop mode in sandbox with friends,ive only had 116 hours of game playing  still feel new and have much to learn but so far i would give this game 8-9/10.i guees the more i play i would definitely have more to say.

fun! really deatailed with npc, zombies, mechs and PvP
very well designed survival system and great QOL changes over the past year, its great fun to return to this game

Been playing on and off for years. Hands down the best survival game ever made. Absolutely love the new/fixed hunting mechanics in Into the Wild!

[h1]Technical Disaster (For Me)[/h1]

I played SCUM for a short time back in [b]2021[/b], but ever since then the game has been completely unplayable for me.

[h2]The Problem[/h2]
[list]
[*]The game launches normally on every device I own
[*]But every single PC ends up with a permanent [b]black screen[/b]
[*]I literally cannot play the game at all
[/list]

[h2]What makes it worse[/h2]
[list]
[*]I’ve spent more time searching for fixes than actually playing the game
[*]Many other players report the exact same issue
[*]None of the suggested solutions worked for me
[*]The problem has existed for years without a proper fix
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

At this point, I’ve completely given up.
The game feels so technically broken and poorly optimized that I honestly never want to launch it again.
I bought game and my computer was within the recommended, now cannot run bc of changes in game..dont buy for long term!

I haven't played this game in over a year because I stopped playing due to hackers. I came back today, and it's still full of them.
I loaded in this morning as a fresh spawn, and after looting a house, a sniper started taking shots at me and then pushed my position. I get that it's a PvP game, and I only had a spear to fight back with, but why was this loser’s character disappearing and reappearing during the fight?
His entire character would vanish and then suddenly reappear in a completely different spot. It made the fight feel ridiculous and unfair.

I would highly recommend this game, it has come a long way in the years that it has been out. The realism of it and the survival aspect of it are unmatched in ways compared to ther games similar to it.

It's a hard game to play, i like the PvE, it gives you adrenaline hunting zombies and looting dangerous areas, there is PvP as well if you are into that, crafting, exploring, surviving etc. Love it. You can spend hours running around. The map is huge

👍 Recommended | 2,000+ Hours Played

After more than 2,000 hours, I'm still logging in regularly.

SCUM is one of the most detailed survival games I've ever played. It tracks so much about your character that every decision matters, and surviving actually feels earned. The game can be brutal, but that's exactly what keeps it interesting.

No matter how much gear I have, I'm always looking for more loot, a better weapon, more supplies, or the next risky bunker run. That constant drive to improve and gear up keeps the gameplay fresh.

The PvP is also incredibly engaging. Every encounter gets the adrenaline going because there's always something on the line. Winning a fight feels rewarding, and losing can be painful—but that's what makes the victories memorable.

I've built bases, lost everything, started over more times than I can count, and still keep coming back.

2,000+ hours later, I'm still having fun. For me, that's the best review I can give. 👍

Game opened to black screen, Averted this by changing to studio drivers. 
Got to play for a few hours to a mix of some enjoyment and some frustration. Then their latest patch just seemed to break the game so i just unnistalled. 
-The game is decent but its not worth the asking price.
-NPCS with God level aim hacks who also never drop anything good over 5% durability
-Avoid the dlc's
-Getting dry is a joke, even standing in a fire, ♥♥♥♥ would dry faster IRL.
-Hunting and fishing are really bad . like really really bad. fishing requires too much and is a massive waste of time when your line instantly snaps and takes 80% of your parts off the rod, and the hunting is non existant. never found a single animal and i spent most of my playtime looking for them..
-Suffers from the whole issue of looting a large town and only finding a rag and a can of food you cant open. but atleast the zombies are not a laughable pathetic joke like in Dayz.
-Respawn mechanic is really dumb. Why and who am i paying to respawn each time ?

Initialy I bought the game (about a year ago) the day it was released and after few minutes decided after a week torefund and went back to Dayz.

Reasons why:
1. Nobody is there to show you tutorials IRL in this scenarios. Dont want to do tutorials, I want to learn not to be forced to learn.

SCUM is a good game with many great features. However, the environment graphics feel outdated, and they made me quit the game twice. Everything in the environment looks too similar — every color blend to each other, making it hard on the eyes and difficult to clearly see things. The trees also look grainy and lack vivid detail. Overall, the environment graphics give an old-school feeling similar to DayZ.

My humble request is to please focus more on improving the environment graphics and visual clarity. If the environment becomes more vivid, clear, and modern-looking, your game could become the number one survival game.

Thank you in advance.

Bought it for 20$ on sale and still feel ripped off. 95% negative experience. Run, lay, run, lay, for 45 minutes just to die the first npc group I cross. 1 bow vs 3-5 guns doesnt work. Have to go to the most dangerous areas with nothing and sneak just to find a backpack only to get smoked by a mech. Prettier than DayZ, but less fun overall. Shouldnt need a gang of friends just to enjoy a game. I give you a 1 out of 10

Terrible, stupid, illogical walking sim.  if it rains for 3 seconds your things get 100% wet and gain 40 pounds, then you throw them on the ground next to a fire to dry them, that takes about 20 minutes, and half the time your things on the ground  ( in a dry place, with no rain or snow ) will go back to 100% wet.  I GAURANTEE you backpacks don't get this wet in real life, nor does it take this long to dry cloths, you can just walk around and get dry! I'm in a dry part of the map.  They do a bunch of junk to make the game 'realistic'' none of this is realistic trust me, except for it being a walking sim....
Calling SCUM finished is laughable. So many broken systems, So much wasted potential.

♥♥♥♥ this game and the fatal errors
This game was gorgeous and I wanted to play this more but constantly losing your progress and never being able to find your corpse makes this game a waste of time to play.

I would not recommend this game yet. That may change if they fix the bugs and death dynamics. The game is filled with bugs. The worst being finding your body after death. If, and that is a big if, you spawn within a reasonable distance of your body chances are you will fight your way against zombies and your own health to find your body is not there. Hint: your gear will be spread out across nearby stashes, all except your good gear. You have a greater chance of it being there if you died on land, and far less of a chance if you died in a building. 
Then, the bugs. You can check your health and find the treatment. The treatment often has nothing to do with your ailment. When it does, just try to get the remedy to take. Good Luck! The worst is intoxication. I became intoxicated from non alcoholic food and drink. It almost healed and increased again twice and took forever to heal. Lastly, the spawning. Often you will respawn far from your body and or base making it impractical to try to return. You are starting off at 0 again until you can gather enough cash, the server has reset the cost lower, or you set solo lower. You'll find you will want to reset many settings to a more realistic setting for game play.

There are so many good things I can say about the game but all is severely overshadowed by inventory management

To call inventory management mechanics atrocious would be a prise. Mid and late game its easy to go out and fill in your backpack with the rest of inventory slots... now try to enjoy moving it like a job from one place to another. No matching drop off, no bulk drop off. Just thinking about any escapade automatically brings my mind to final stage of managing inventory which automatically and brutally kills the joy. I feel more joy thinking about my 9-5 job than this inventory management. 

PROS: none
CONS: everything

If you like inventory management games, I meant jobs, then this game if for you!
Fun Game with a lot going on. Not for the faint of heart definetly  has a steep learning curve but once you get it, its worth the struggle.
